title: Serum V2 — Filter
summary: Shaping brightness with Serum's filter — which type to use, where to set cutoff and resonance for UK garage sounds.
steps: Enable the FILTER by clicking the power button in the FILTER section header — it's off on Init Preset
Click the filter type dropdown and choose 'MG Low 24' — this is the standard choice for pads. Smooth, warm, musical.
Set CUTOFF to ~55–65% of the dial for a pad (roughly 1–2kHz). This removes harsh upper harmonics while keeping presence.
Set RES (resonance) to 5–15% for pads — just enough to give the filter a gentle character without any squeal. Higher resonance adds a peak at the cutoff frequency.
For a sub bass: disable the filter entirely (power button off). Sub bass needs no filtering — keep the signal as clean as possible.
For a stab: set CUTOFF higher (75–85%) so the stab stays bright. Fast attack sounds benefit from an open filter.

To add filter movement: drag ENV 2 onto the CUTOFF knob. Set ENV 2 attack to match ENV 1, decay 500ms, amount moderate. The filter opens as the note hits and closes as it decays — classic pad movement.
Try 'Dirty Low' instead of 'MG Low 24' for a bass sound that needs edge — it saturates slightly at the filter stage.
tips: MG Low 24 = smooth and warm. Dirty Low = gritty. PRO Low (V2) = modern and precise. For pads, start with MG Low 24.
If the filter is on but nothing seems to be changing, check that CUTOFF isn't at 100% — fully open filter has no effect
Resonance above 50% starts squealing. Keep it below 20% for pad work unless you want an audible filter character.
The FILTER has its own ENV routing button (small icon next to the cutoff knob) — click it to quickly assign ENV 2 to cutoff
